steps.title: Interpret the conditional probability
steps.content:
Given that the flower is a tulip, we need to find what fraction of the tulips are from Site A. This is expressed as:
$$P(\text{Site A | Tulip}) = \frac{\text{Tulips from Site A}}{\text{Total tulips}}$$

steps.title: Extract values and calculate
steps.content:
From the Tulip row of the table: Site A has 35 tulips out of 50 total.
$$P(\text{Site A | Tulip}) = \frac{35}{50} = \frac{7}{10} = 0.7$$
